mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate indicates a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with economic expansion
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE is below its 5-year moving average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong retail sales growth indicates consumer confidence
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significant from zero (p-value > 0.1) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high but the Federal Funds Rate remains low, it signals an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which can fuel asset price appreciation as real rates are suppressed, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low real interest rates support equity valuations
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (less than 0.5%) in periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings are boosted by higher output and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified during early-to-mid cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 20-day returns turns negative during identified early-to-mid cycle expansions over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of housing starts indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which has a broad positive impact on the economy and corporate earnings, supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a key indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative when HOUST is above its 12-month moving average for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly decline in non-farm payrolls signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:significant job losses precede economic downturns
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 in a month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP growth and retail sales growth are strong, it indicates broad-based economic expansion driven by robust consumer and business demand, which strongly supports corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and retail sales are primary indicators of demand
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative when both GDPC1 annualized growth is > 2% and RSAFS month-over-month growth is > 0.3% for more than 3 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing profit margins and dampening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:rate hikes typically precede market corrections
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months during a tightening cycle.
